mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer income, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or positive returns occur in >60% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand recovery, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 20d returns in >50% of occurrences during recovery phases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during late-cycle or recessionary regimes, or 5d returns are negative in >55% of rising INDPRO months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation readings above 0.4 percent force markets to price in aggressive future monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns remain positive in >60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, which supports consumer spending and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 60d returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction effect fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate profit margins, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20d forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in <45% of growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative three-month decline in industrial production exceeding 2.0 percent signals severe supply-side contraction and weakening business investment, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal yields positive 60d returns in >55% of triggered instances, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.
